tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and high urine protein, ketones, and glucose, indicating possible nephropathy and glycosuria despite low-normal blood glucose and low HbA1c. Urine is dilute, brown, cloudy, and ammoniacal, raising concern for renal or urinary tract pathology. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further renal assessment and clinical correlation for possible chronic kidney disease and underlying metabolic disturbances.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
